title: Sarit E. Batner
content:
Partner | Chair of the Board of Partners
People.ContactEmail sbatner@mccarthy.ca
- University of Toronto
As one of Canada’s most renowned litigators, Sarit provides clients with the certainty they’ll achieve the best possible result in any dispute.
Sarit Batner is a partner in our Litigation and Dispute Resolution Group and Chair of the firm’s Board of Partners. With a singular reputation for courtroom mastery and impeccable litigation strategy, Sarit has successfully resolved some of Canada’s most complex corporate-commercial disputes, earning fellowship invitations to the American College of Trial Lawyers, the International Academy of Trial Lawyers, and the Litigation Counsel of America. She is recipient of the prestigious Award for Excellence in Civil Litigation from the Ontario Bar Association and the Douglas K. Laidlaw Medal for Excellence in Advocacy from the Advocates’ Society.
Sarit’s body of work is extensive and covers a diverse range of industries, including pharmaceuticals, healthcare, transport, renewables, infrastructure, and automotives. She advises on breach of contract, securities, shareholder disputes, taxation, oppression, professional negligence, breaches of fiduciary duty, and provincial cost-recovery legislation.
Benchmark Litigation has recognized Sarit as one of its Top 50 Trial Lawyers in Canada for nearly a decade, owing to her combination of instinctive understanding of the law and civil procedure and a razor-sharp ability to quickly comprehend clients’ industries and unique challenges. In addition to representing the biggest global and domestic companies on their Canadian disputes, Sarit advises corporate directors, doctors, engineers, accountants, and lawyers in a wide variety of professional liability and fiduciary matters before courts and tribunals. She is often brought onto files for her meticulous cross-examination, effectively striking at the core testimonies of opposing parties with compelling oral presentation and deep comprehension of the subject matter at hand. Her expertise and responsiveness in emergency injunctions further make her an indispensable resource when preserving her clients’ sensitive interests.
Selected trials/hearings:
- Infrastructure Ontario – in its role as procurement advisor to the City of Ottawa regarding a public inquiry into the $2-billion Ottawa Light Rail Transit proposal.
- Unity Health Toronto – ongoing – civil litigation defending claims from Zurich and others to rescind construction bonds issued for the St. Michael’s Hospital redevelopment project.
- Access Copyright – claims against school boards in eight Canadian provinces and three territories for unpaid K-12 copying tariff royalties.
- The Ontario Ministry of Transportation and Infrastructure Ontario – regarding the expansion of Highway 427, Canada’s second-busiest highway.
- Nigig Power – defence against claimed entitlement to equity interest or equivalent compensation for advisory services regarding a wind farm owned by Henley Inlet First Nation.
- Metrolinx – legal challenge, including multiple injunctions, to build the Osgoode Station as part of the $19-billion Ontario line.
- Bell Canada, Telus, and Rogers – multiple patent infringement disputes regarding IPTV and wireless networks.
- Frank Stronach – successful resolution of litigation over control of the Stronach Group, a complex collection of trusts, corporations, and other entities created out of Frank Stronach’s multi-billion dollar auto-parts business.
- MerckWyeth (vs Pfizer) – successful patent trial re: Prevnar (pneumococcal vaccines).
- KF Capital – successful motion re: corporate indemnities in context of breach of fiduciary duty claims.
- Aphria –application re: corporate indemnities in context of market manipulation of securities claim.
- Dibble – successful multi week medical negligence trial re: aortic mass.

### Appellate experience
Sarit’s appellate practice is equally remarkable. In addition to frequent appearances at provincial courts of appeal, she has been lead counsel before the Supreme Court of Canada, including in precedent-setting cases affecting civil procedure, access to justice, and contractual interpretation. With ample experience at trial and on appeal, Sarit works closely with clients to map out the full life cycle of a dispute, anticipating challenges, identifying novel opportunities to conclude matters, and confidently advocating before court or proposing a settlement that minimizes undesirable outcomes. Her ability to marshal lean and nimble teams with a precise mix of the needed legal acumen ensure matters are resolved as efficiently and effectively as possible.
- Romandale Farms Limited – appeal considering issues including good faith, estoppel, frustration, mistake, and specific performance.
- Grasshopper Solar – appeal considering breach of contract issues including time is of the essence clauses and estoppel.
- York University – appeal re: jurisdiction of university tribunal over matters of student discipline.
- Mary’s General Hospital – successful appeal from successful injunction re: removal of life support.
- Romandale Farms Limited – successfully quashing appeal for want of jurisdiction.
- East West Plastics – successful appeal following a long trial (Commercial List) involving oppression remedy and breach of fiduciary duty.
- Merck & Co. Inc. – successfully upholding trial decision granting Merck over $200-million in damages for patent infringement.
- AbbVie Corporation – successful motion opposing stay of proceedings before Federal Court of Appeal.
- Hryniak – lead counsel in Supreme Court of Canada appeal concerning summary judgment and access to justice; arising out of landmark decision from Ontario Court of Appeal setting the law on summary judgment in Ontario.

### Arbitral experience and public agency actions
While Sarit is well known for her courtroom work, her expertise is also sought after in highly confidential arbitral proceedings and public inquiries. Clients defending multi-million and multi-billion dollar disputes that go to the core of their business retain Sarit to forcefully defend and protect their interests with bet-the-farm matters in high-pressure negotiations. This includes traditional private arbitrations and high-profile public inquires and other actions by government agencies, including the novel use of cost-recovery legislation. With due regard for the public and commercial implications of such proceedings, Sarit offers clients the confidence that their most important trade secrets and business goals are protected.
Selected arbitral and other actions:
- Confidential mining client – highly confidential and time-sensitive international securities arbitration valued at over $2-billion.
- Infrastructure Ontario – public inquiry regarding the $2-billion Ottawa Light Rail Transit proposal.

### Leadership at McCarthy Tétrault and in the legal profession
As Chair of McCarthy Tétrault’s Board of Partners, Sarit leads at the intersection of business and law, where she advances core priorities for the firm and the legal profession. Promoting women and equity seeking groups, ensuring excellence and professionalism, and a commitment to innovation are all features of her leadership. As clients move into new markets and the speed of global commerce increases, Sarit is also focused on curating, harmonizing, and modernizing McCarthy Tétrault’s relationship with partner law firms around the world and elevating our legal talent on an international platform.
Other examples of Sarit’s commitment to the legal profession include regular speaking engagements at the Ontario Bar Association, Canadian Bar Association, the Advocates’ Society, the Law Society of Ontario, and other professional organizations. Having mentored several “Rising Stars” and “Ones to Watch,” Sarit continues to guide the brightest emerging legal talent in Canada. She is a contributing author to Virtual Advocacy: Litigating from a Distance and Civil Procedure and Practice in Ontario . Sarit also sits on the board of Stronach International.
Sarit obtained a B.Sc. with Distinction in Mathematics and was awarded the Gold Medal in Sciences from the University of Western Ontario in 1995. She obtained a JD from the University of Toronto in 1998 and was called to the Ontario bar in 2000.
